parsed_review.summary: CP00-421 is a clean, internally-consistent descriptor-only continuation of the RP13 payments-core slice, following the established and well-tested pattern of the 29 prior packs. Pack baseline matches across manifest, review-baseline, live closeout-pack-plan, registry PACK_BINDING, contract, and README: risk A, 10 units, range RP13.P09.M07.S12-RP13.P09.M08.S01, deliverable distribution {implementation:8, claude_review:2}, micro-title distribution {"Test And Golden Case Set":9, "Hermes Evidence Packet":1}. M07 (S12-S20, 9 rows) plus M08 head (S01, 1 row) completes the planned 10 units; the M07 micro-phase is fully covered across CP419/CP420/CP421 (1+10+9=20). The pack-level production_ready claim is FALSE (status implemented_pending_review, implementation_layer descriptor, runtime_ready false) and is well-supported: it does not over-claim. The validator's production_ready_candidate:true is scoped to descriptor validity (coverage.valid && slice.valid), not enterprise production readiness. Claude's read-only/non-final-approver boundary is enforced in descriptor authority_boundary (claude_is_final_approval:false), no-write attestation (promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false), tests, and the contract validator. Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (receipt_shape descriptor_only_payments_p09_test_hermes_slice_no_write; emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false) with no product runtime. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are extensively attested in freezeCp421Result and asserted by tests and the validator, which also deep-equals the contract p09_test_hermes_slice_descriptor against the generated descriptor. Review-hardening evidence is valid: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1432B stdout, well-formed JSON), this is the first review attempt with no prior auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/tool-shaped evidence to reject. Changed-file scope is correct (7 modified sources plus untracked docs/closeout-packs/cp00-421/); unrelated dirty files are properly excluded and no merge-conflict markers exist in the changed files. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One P3 transparency note: the post-review evidence artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is expected at review time since this review produces them and the later hardening steps generate the rest. As read-only reviewer (not final approver), I record PASS; final enterprise-trust closure remains a human responsibility and the pack must still complete its post-review hardening and final closeout validation before commit.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts pending generation (expected, not a defect)
findings.details: The manifest declares docs/closeout-packs/cp00-421/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json as required_pack_artifacts/evidence_refs, but they are not present at review time. This is correct by design: this read-only review produces claude-review-result.json, and adjudication.md + construction-inspection.json are generated by the later finding_adjudication and construction_inspection steps of manifest.pack_level_claude_review.hardening_sequence (also enumerated as post-review gated commands in command-evidence.json notes). Because they are pending, the 'adjudication' and 'inspection' inputs to the production_ready support chain could not be independently verified during this review.
findings.recommendation: Ensure all three post-review artifacts are generated and pass npm run closeout-pack:validate, with final closeout validation and git diff --check --cached green, before committing/closing CP00-421. Do not mark the pack closed until they exist and validate.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected/by-design pending state; no implementation change required. Verify artifact presence and validation at the final closeout gate.
